channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: 'Chicken wars': France's highly political fast-food fight
item.description: In this edition of Entre Nous, we take a look at France's "chicken wars", or the fast-food fight over a roasted chicken chain that's become highly political. We also dive into the nation's love of fast food, and in particular chicken. Finally, we discuss some of the many expressions in French that pay tribute to the most Gaulois of birds: the Gallus gallus, or "le poulet".
item.dc:creator: Solange MOUGIN

item.title: 'We've been poisoned': Babies exposed to pesticides in the womb
item.description: Rates of cancer, birth defects and cognitive disorders are on the rise among French children. The use of pesticides has been recognised as a major risk to mothers and their babies, including in utero. Since 2020, victims of pesticides can request compensation from a government fund for their illness. But families say authorities are not doing enough to protect the most vulnerable. Our Down to Earth team reports.
item.dc:creator: Valérie DEKIMPE

item.title: James L. Brooks on leading ladies and the political game
item.description: After six decades in Hollywood, James L. Brooks' latest heroine "Ella McCay" is a natural successor to his pioneering star of the small screen, "Mary Tyler Moore". This new, 21st-century heroine is juggling a political career with family drama, and her creator tells us why Ella's approach to social and political change is at odds with way democracy is being managed today in the US.
item.dc:creator: Olivia SALAZAR-WINSPEAR

item.title: REPLAY: Pentagon chief Hegseth says ceasefire with Iran not over
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said at a press conference on Tuesday that ​a US operation to protect commercial ships from Iran in the Strait of Hormuz is temporary, Washington is not looking ​for a ‌fight and the ceasefire with Iran ⁠is still in effect. "The ceasefire is not over," Hegseth told reporters. Watch hsi press conference in full above.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Two dead as car rams crowd in Germany's Leipzig
item.description: Two ​people were killed and three others were seriously injured on Monday when a car drove into a central pedestrian zone of the eastern German city of Leipzig, Mayor Burkhard Jung ​said, the ‌latest in a spate of such incidents in recent years ⁠in Germany. Police arrested the driver, identifying him as a 33-year-old man with German citizenship.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Rebel groups in Mali capture a drone control station
item.description: Two rebel groups seized the city of Kidal in Mali on April 26. One of the sites they captured houses a ground control station for drones once operated by the Malian Army.
item.dc:creator: The FRANCE 24 Observers

item.title: Iran Nobel winner Mohammadi 'between life and death' after hospitalisation
item.description: Jailed Iranian Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi is fighting for her life after being hospitalised under guard for the last five days with a heart condition, her supporters said on Tuesday.
item.source: © File photo from the Narges Foundation via AP
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Human-to-human hantavirus transmission suspected on cruise ship
item.description: The World Health Organization said Tuesday it suspected that hantavirus may have spread between people on a cruise ship, with three dead among seven confirmed and suspected cases. "We do believe that there may be some human-to-human transmission that is happening among the really close contacts," the WHO's epidemic and pandemic preparedness and prevention director Maria Van Kerkhove told reporters.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Thailand's monkey business: Pharmaceutical labs rely on primate testing
item.description: Many pharmaceutical giants use monkeys to test drugs and vaccines. But since China banned the export of primates in 2020, prices have skyrocketed, fuelilng an international black market. At the heart of this illegal trafficking are Cambodia, Thailand and Laos. FRANCE 24's Justin McCurry, Théo Collet, Antoine Morel and Cyrille Charpentier report.
item.dc:creator: Justin McCURRY

item.title: Romania's pro-EU coalition government collapses after prime minister loses no-confidence vote
item.description: Romania’s pro-European coalition collapsed Tuesday after its parliament voted in a no-confidence motion against Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan, triggering a fresh period of turmoil in the EU and NATO country bordering Ukraine less than a year after the coalition was sworn in.
item.source: © Octav Ganea, Inquam Photos via Reuters (file photo)
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: NollywoodWeek Film Festival co-founder Serge Noukoué on its 13th edition
item.description: The 13th edition of the NollywoodWeek Film Festival is back in Paris, with a theme of "Voyage", taking audiences to Nigeria and beyond. It's an industry worth over $6 billion and growing fast, but it's also at a turning point, caught between global expansion and the pressures of streaming, technology and who gets to tell its stories. Serge Noukoué, co-founder of the NollywoodWeek Film Festival, spoke to us in Perspective.
item.dc:creator: Yinka OYETADE

item.title: Modi’s party wins control of India's West Bengal for first time
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s ruling Hindu nationalist party has wrested control of the opposition stronghold of West Bengal in a key state election, though opposition parties have sharply criticized the polls after the Election Commission removed millions of voters from electoral rolls.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: German minesweeper sets sail for possible Hormuz mission
item.description: A German minesweeper has set sail for the Mediterranean this Monday in preparation for a possible deployment in the Strait of Hormuz. Berlin has said it is prepared to contribute to ensure the freedom of navigation in the waterway in the Middle East, where Tehran has blocked traffic in retaliation after the United States launched airstrikes against Tehran at the end of February. But Germany said it will act only if certain conditions are met.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Stamps celebrate 100 years of America's famed Route 66
item.description: Route 66, the 2,448-mile-long road that cuts through the United States turns 100 years old this year. To celebrate the anniversary the US Postal Service has commissioned photographer David J. Schwartz, who has spent two decades documenting the iconic route, to produce a special series of Route 66 stamps. The eight new postmarks capture something unique in each of the states the road passes through.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ugandans slam bill as mimicking Russian 'foreign agents' law
item.description: A proposed law critics say is designed to crush dissent is sparking growing outrage in Uganda. The bill seeks to criminalise anything promoting the "interests of a foreigner" and would allow anyone receiving money from abroad to be labelled a "foreign agent". In a press conference Monday, civil society organisations said the legislation replicates similars used in Russia.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: UAE says oil facility hit by Iranian drone strike as attacks resume
item.description: The United Arab Emirates said Monday it came under attack by Iran for the first time since a fragile ceasefire took hold in early April, accusing Iran of sparking a fire at a key oil facility in a drone strike. The UAE Defense Ministry also said Iran had launched four cruise missiles, with three shot down and one falling into the sea.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'The highest tensions have been' as US, Iran trade fire in Hormuz
item.description: The US military said it fired on Iranian forces and sank six small boats targeting civilian ships as it moved to reopen the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, while the United Arab Emirates said it had come under attack from Iran for the first time since a fragile ceasefire took hold in early April. France 24's correspondent in Tehran, Reza Sayah, says tensions in the Middle East are at the highest they've been since the ceasefire began.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Stars embrace art-inspired fashion at Met Gala
item.description: Celebrities including supermodel Heidi Klum and singer Beyoncé walked the carpet at the Met Gala on Monday — many of them unrecognizable as they leaned heavily into this year’s theme, ‘Fashion is art’.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Airline's plan for fuel surcharge raises questions of legality
item.description: As jet fuel prices continue to skyrocket, some airlines, such as Spanish budget carrier Volotea, have decided to add a fuel surcharge on tickets, even on those that have already been purchased. But while the idea may help offset rising costs, some have questioned its legality.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France’s Macron sings 'La Bohème' with the Armenian President
item.description: Two presidents and a prime minister in an impromptu musical performance … It was an unusual scene that unfolded on Monday, in Yerevan, Armenia: French President Emmanuel Macron, who is concluding a state visit to the country on Tuesday, joined in the singing of La Bohème by Charles Aznavour during the state dinner held in his honour.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Vijay: From Tamil Nadu's biggest film star to Indian political leader
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – Tuesday, May 5: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi's party is celebrating an "historic" state election win in West Bengal, while Vijay, one of the most famous Tamil film stars, just won the state election in Tamil Nadu with his upstart political party. We look at reactions from the Indian press about these two victories. Also, we discover the winners of the Pulitzer Prizes and check out the best and worst dressed attendees at the Met Gala.
item.dc:creator: Dheepthika LAURENT

item.title: WHO confirms two hantavirus cases aboard cruise ship, suspects human transmission
item.description: The World Health Organization on Tuesday said it had confirmed two hantavirus cases and suspected five others among people on a cruise ship stuck off Cape Verde. The WHO added that there may be some human-to-human transmission ​happening ‌between ⁠close contacts on board the ‌stricken cruise ship, though the risk to the wider ⁠public remained low.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Jailed French sports journalist drops Algeria appeal in hopes of presidential pardon
item.description: French journalist Christophe Gleizes has dropped an appeal against his imprisonment in Algeria in the hopes of securing his release through a presidential pardon, his family said on Tuesday. Gleizes was arrested in 2024 at the height of diplomatic tensions between France and Algeria.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Crooning diplomacy: France, Armenia leaders unite over shared icon Charles Aznavour
item.description: French president Emmanuel Macron donned his crooning hat during a state dinner with Armenian leaders on Monday, with a rendition of a song by late Charles Aznavour, who often served as a cultural bridge between the two countries. Often called France's Frank Sinatra, Aznavour was born in France to Armenian immigrants and went on to become a revered figure in the two countries.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
